MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-22-2025 Assigned To Trial Court

MOTION TO CONTINUE COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-17-2025 Text Ruling

Motion To Compel Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant Aumet, Inc.'S Further Reponses To Requests For Admission, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Requests For Production Of Douments, Set One
Off Calendar Sep-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MOUSTAFA SHAABAN Motion To Compel Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant Aumet, Inc.'S Further Reponses To Requests For Admission, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Requests For Production Of Douments, Set One

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MOUSTAFA SHAABAN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff And Cross-Defendant Aumet, Inc.'s Further Reponses To Requests For Admission, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Requests For Production Of Douments, Set One.

Defendant's Motion to Compel Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant Aumet, Inc.'s Further Reponses is DENIED. First, defendant filed an improper stacked discovery motion. The single motion seeks further responses to the requests for admissions, special interrogatories, and requests for production of documents. Movant should have filed three motions. (See CRC 3.112(a), (c) [format of motions].) Second, movant's separate statements do not comply with CRC 3.1345(c) ["A separate statement is a separate document filed and served with the discovery motion that provides all the information necessary to understand each discovery request and all the responses to it that are at issue."] Here, plaintiff objected to the preface, definitions, and instructions in defendant's discovery requests. The separate statements do not include that information. Moreover, defendant failed to provide copies of the discovery requests so the court has no way to even evaluate the merits of these objections.

The request for sanctions is denied.

A proposed order is submitted prior to the hearing.

Judge: John M. True, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Granting Defendant Leave To Defend; **First Appearance Fees Paid**
Off Calendar Aug-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MOUSTAFA SHAABAN'S Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Granting Defendant Leave To Defend.

Defendant Moustafa Shaaban's motion for order setting aside and vacating default and granting him leave to defend, which is unopposed, is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The court orders set aside and vacated the default and any related judgment, and grants Shabaan leave to defend. The court denies the request to dismiss the matter for lack of jurisdiction without prejudice to presentation of the issue in the course of Shabaan's defense. The request is improper during the period in which the requesting party is in default.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported.
